(*                       Some Temporal Logic Proof Rules                   *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* We now state some temporal logic proof rules that are used in the       *)
(* liveness proof.  Some version of these rules will eventually be added   *)
(* to the TLAPS module.                                                    *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The first rule is the lattice rule.  To state it, we define             *)
(* WellFounded(S, LT) to assert that the relation LT is a well-founded     *)
(* "less-than" relation on the set S.  This means that there is no         *)
(* infinite sequence of elements of S, each of which is less than the      *)
(* previous one.  We represent a relation the way mathematicians generally *)
(* do, as a set of ordered pairs.  In this case <<s, t>> \in LT means that *)
(* s is less than t.                                                       *)
(***************************************************************************)
WellFounded(S, LT) == ~ \E f \in [Nat -> S] : 
                           \A i \in Nat : <<f[i+1], f[i]>> \in LT

(***************************************************************************)
(* We now define ProperSubsetRel(S) to be the relation on a set S such     *)
(* that <<U, V>> \in S if and only if U and V are subsets of S with U a    *)
(* proper subset of V.  We then state without proof the result that, if S  *)
(* is a finite set, then ProperSubsetRel(S) is a well-founded relation on  *)
(* S.                                                                      *)
(***************************************************************************)
ProperSubsetRel(S) == 
  {r \in (SUBSET S) \X (SUBSET S) : /\ r[1] \subseteq r[2]
                                    /\ r[1] # r[2] }     
                                                     
THEOREM SubsetWellFounded ==
           \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => WellFounded(SUBSET S, ProperSubsetRel(S))
PROOF OMITTED

(***************************************************************************)
(* Here is our statement of the Lattice Rule, which is discussed in        *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*    AUTHOR = "Leslie Lamport",                                           *)
(*    TITLE = "The Temporal Logic of Actions",                             *)
(*    JOURNAL = toplas,                                                    *)
(*    volume = 16,                                                         *)
(*    number = 3,                                                          *)
(*    YEAR = 1994,                                                         *)
(*    month = may,                                                         *)
(*    PAGES = "872--923"                                                   *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM LatticeRule ==  ASSUME NEW S, NEW LT, WellFounded(S, LT),
                               NEW TEMPORAL P(_), NEW TEMPORAL Q
                        PROVE  /\ Q \/ (\E i \in S : P(i))
                               /\ \A i \in S : 
                                     P(i) ~> (Q \/ \E j \in S : (<<j, i>> \in LT) /\ P(j))
                               => ((\E i \in S : P(i)) ~> Q)
PROOF OMITTED

/opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps/FiniteSets.tla:9:IsFiniteSet(S) == 
/opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps/FiniteSets.tla:16:Cardinality(S) ==

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_VT3.tla
@@
 THEOREM VT3 == Spec => C"'!Spec 
 PROOF
-<1>1. Init => C!Init
-  BY QuorumNonEmpty DEF Init, C!Init, chosen, ChosenIn, VotedFor
-<1>2. VInv => C!Inv
-  BY VT1 DEF VInv, C!Inv, C!TypeOK, chosen
-<1>3. VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars => [C"'!Next]_C!vars
-  BY VT1, VT1Prime DEF VInv, C!Next, C!vars
-<1>4. Spec => []C!Inv
-  BY <1>2, VT2 DEF Spec
-<1>5. Spec => [][C!Next]_C!vars
-  BY <1>3, VT2 DEF Spec
-<1>6. QED
-  BY <1>1, <1>5 DEF Spec, C!Spec
+<1>1. Init => chosen = {}
+  <2>1. ASSUME Init
+          PROVE  chosen = {}
+    <3>1. '"\\A v \\in Value : ~(\\E b \\in Ballot : ChosenIn(b, v))
+      BY <2>1, QuorumNonEmpty DEF Init, ChosenIn, VotedFor
+    <3>2. QED
+      BY <3>1 DEF chosen
+<1>2. Init => C"'!Init
+  BY <1>1 DEF C!Init
+<1>3. VInv => chosen '"\\subseteq Value
+  BY DEF chosen
+<1>4. VInv => (\\A v, w \\in chosen : v = w)
+  BY VT1 DEF VInv
+<1>5. VInv => C"'!Inv
+  BY <1>3, <1>4, C!EmptySetCardinality, C!SingletonCardinality
+     DEF C!Inv, C!TypeOK
+<1>6. VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars => [C"'!Next]_C!vars
+  <2>1. ASSUME NEW CONSTANT v,
+                VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars,
+                chosen' = {v}
+          PROVE  chosen = {}
+    BY <1>4, VT1Prime DEF VInv
+  <2>2. QED
+    BY <2>1 DEF C"'!Next, C!vars
+<1>7. Spec => []VInv
+  BY VT2
+<1>8. Spec => []C!Inv
+  BY <1>5, <1>7 (* PTL *)
+<1>9. Spec => [][C!Next]_C!vars
+  BY <1>6, <1>7 DEF Spec (* PTL *)
+<1>10. QED
+  BY <1>2, <1>9 DEF Spec, C!Spec
 
 ===============================================================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps --cleanfp --nofp VoteProof_VT3.tla'

558	(* The following theorem asserts that the safety properties of the voting  *)
   559	(* algorithm (specified by formula Spec) of this module implement the      *)
   560	(* consensus safety specification Spec of module Consensus under the       *)
   561	(* substitution (refinement mapping) of the INSTANCE statement.            *)
   562	(***************************************************************************)
   563	THEOREM VT3 == Spec => C!Spec 
   564	PROOF
   565	<1>1. Init => chosen = {}
   566	  <2>1. ASSUME Init
   567	          PROVE  chosen = {}
   568	    <3>1. \A v \in Value : ~(\E b \in Ballot : ChosenIn(b, v))
   569	      BY <2>1, QuorumNonEmpty DEF Init, ChosenIn, VotedFor
   570	    <3>2. QED
   571	      BY <3>1 DEF chosen
   572	<1>2. Init => C!Init
   573	  BY <1>1 DEF C!Init
   574	<1>3. VInv => chosen \subseteq Value
   575	  BY DEF chosen
   576	<1>4. VInv => (\A v, w \in chosen : v = w)
   577	  BY VT1 DEF VInv
   578	<1>5. VInv => C!Inv
   579	  BY <1>3, <1>4, C!EmptySetCardinality, C!SingletonCardinality
   580	     DEF C!Inv, C!TypeOK
   581	<1>6. VInv /\ VInv' /\ [Next]_vars => [C!Next]_C!vars
   582	  <2>1. ASSUME NEW CONSTANT v,
   583	                VInv /\ VInv' /\ [Next]_vars,
   584	                chosen' = {v}
   585	          PROVE  chosen = {}
   586	    BY <1>4, VT1Prime DEF VInv
   587	  <2>2. QED
   588	    BY <2>1 DEF C!Next, C!vars
   589	<1>7. Spec => []VInv
   590	  BY VT2
   591	<1>8. Spec => []C!Inv
   592	  BY <1>5, <1>7 (* PTL *)
   593	<1>9. Spec => [][C!Next]_C!vars
   594	  BY <1>6, <1>7 DEF Spec (* PTL *)
   595	<1>10. QED
   596	  BY <1>2, <1>9 DEF Spec, C!Spec
   597	
   598	===============================================================================

/bin/bash -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_VT3.tla
@@
-<1>1. Init => chosen = {}
-  <2>1. ASSUME Init
-          PROVE  chosen = {}
+<1>1. Init => chosen = {}
+  <2> SUFFICES ASSUME Init
+                PROVE  chosen = {}
+    OBVIOUS
     <3>1. \\A v \\in Value : ~(\\E b \\in Ballot : ChosenIn(b, v))
-      BY <2>1, QuorumNonEmpty DEF Init, ChosenIn, VotedFor
+      BY <2>, QuorumNonEmpty DEF Init, ChosenIn, VotedFor
     <3>2. QED
       BY <3>1 DEF chosen
@@
 <1>6. VInv /\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars => [C"'!Next]_C!vars
-  <2>1. ASSUME NEW CONSTANT v,
-                VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars,
-                chosen' = {v}
-          PROVE  chosen = {}
+  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW CONSTANT v,
+                         VInv /\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars,
+                         chosen' = {v}
+                   PROVE  chosen = {}
+    OBVIOUS
     BY <1>4, VT1Prime DEF VInv
*** End Patch
PATCH
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps --cleanfp --nofp VoteProof_VT3.tla"

item.text: The issue is just proof grammar: nested substeps need an explicit local `PROOF`. I’ll patch that and continue.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_VT3.tla
@@
 <1>1. Init => chosen = {}
+  PROOF
   <2> SUFFICES ASSUME Init
                 PROVE  chosen = {}
@@
 <1>6. VInv /\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars => [C"'!Next]_C!vars
+  PROOF
   <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW CONSTANT v,
                          VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars,
*** End Patch
PATCH
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps --cleanfp --nofp VoteProof_VT3.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_VT3.tla
@@
 <1>1. Init => chosen = {}
+  PROOF
   <2> SUFFICES ASSUME Init
                 PROVE  chosen = {}
@@
 <1>6. VInv /\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars => [C"'!Next]_C!vars
+  PROOF
   <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW CONSTANT v,
                          VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars,
*** End Patch
PATCH
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps --cleanfp --nofp VoteProof_VT3.tla"

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_VT3.tla
@@
 THEOREM VT3 == Spec => C"'!Spec 
 PROOF
-<1>1. Init => chosen = {}
-  PROOF
-  <2> SUFFICES ASSUME Init
-                PROVE  chosen = {}
-    OBVIOUS
-    <3>1. '"\\A v \\in Value : ~(\\E b \\in Ballot : ChosenIn(b, v))
-      BY <2>, QuorumNonEmpty DEF Init, ChosenIn, VotedFor
-    <3>2. QED
-      BY <3>1 DEF chosen
-<1>2. Init => C"'!Init
-  BY <1>1 DEF C!Init
-<1>3. VInv => chosen '"\\subseteq Value
-  BY DEF chosen
-<1>4. VInv => (\\A v, w \\in chosen : v = w)
-  BY VT1 DEF VInv
-<1>5. VInv => C"'!Inv
-  BY <1>3, <1>4, C!EmptySetCardinality, C!SingletonCardinality
-     DEF C!Inv, C!TypeOK
-<1>6. VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars => [C"'!Next]_C!vars
-  PROOF
-  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW CONSTANT v,
-                         VInv /'"\\ VInv' /\\ [Next]_vars,
-                         chosen' = {v}
-                   PROVE  chosen = {}
-    OBVIOUS
-    BY <1>4, VT1Prime DEF VInv
-  <2>2. QED
-    BY <2>1 DEF C"'!Next, C!vars
-<1>7. Spec => []VInv
-  BY VT2
-<1>8. Spec => []C!Inv
-  BY <1>5, <1>7 (* PTL *)
-<1>9. Spec => [][C!Next]_C!vars
-  BY <1>6, <1>7 DEF Spec (* PTL *)
-<1>10. QED
-  BY <1>2, <1>9 DEF Spec, C!Spec

+  BY VT2

+<1>8. Spec => [][C!Next]_C!vars
+  BY <1>5, <1>6, PTL DEF Spec
+<1>9. QED
+  BY <1>1, <1>8 DEF Spec, C!Spec

Isa == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle") }*)
IsaT(X) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; timeout:@) }*)
IsaM(X) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:@) }*)
IsaMT(X,Y) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:@; timeout:@) }*)

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following theorem expresses the (useful implication of the) law of  *)
(* set extensionality, which can be written as                             *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*    THEOREM  \A S, T : (S = T) <=> (\A x : (x \in S) <=> (x \in T))      *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Theorem SetExtensionality is sometimes required by the SMT backend for  *)
(* reasoning about sets. It is usually counterproductive to include        *)
(* theorem SetExtensionality in a BY clause for the Zenon or Isabelle      *)
(* backends. Instead, use the pragma IsaWithSetExtensionality to instruct  *)
(* the Isabelle backend to use the rule of set extensionality.             *)
(***************************************************************************)
IsaWithSetExtensionality == TRUE
           (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)")}*)

THEOREM SetExtensionality == \A S,T : (\A x : x \in S <=> x \in T) => S = T
OBVIOUS

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following theorem is needed to deduce NotInSetS \notin SetS from    *)
(* the definition                                                          *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*   NotInSetS == CHOOSE v : v \notin SetS                                 *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM NoSetContainsEverything == \A S : \E x : x \notin S
OBVIOUS (*{by (isabelle "(auto intro: inIrrefl)")}*)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The cardinality of a finite set S is 0 iff S is empty.                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_EmptySet ==
  /\ IsFiniteSet({})
  /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
  /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => (Cardinality(S) = 0 <=> S = {})


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* If S is finite, so are S \cup {x} and S \ {x}.                          *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_AddElement ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW x, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
         /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) =
            IF x \in S THEN Cardinality(S) ELSE Cardinality(S)+1

THEOREM FS_RemoveElement ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW x, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
         /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) =
            IF x \in S THEN Cardinality(S)-1 ELSE Cardinality(S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* In particular, a singleton set is finite.                               *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Singleton ==
  /\ \A x : IsFiniteSet({x}) /\ Cardinality({x}) = 1
  /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => (Cardinality(S) = 1 <=> \E x: S = {x})


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)

IsWellFoundedOn(R,S)
        PROVE  IsWellFoundedOn(R,T)
BY DEF IsWellFoundedOn


LEMMA IsWellFoundedOnSubrelation ==
       ASSUME NEW S, NEW R, NEW RR, RR \cap (S \X S) \subseteq R,
              IsWellFoundedOn(R,S)
       PROVE  IsWellFoundedOn(RR,S)
<1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW f \in [Nat -> S],
                      \A n \in Nat : <<f[n+1], f[n]>> \in RR
               PROVE  FALSE
  BY DEF IsWellFoundedOn
<1>2. \A n \in Nat : <<f[n+1], f[n]>> \in RR \cap (S \X S)
  BY <1>1
<1>. QED  BY <1>2 DEF IsWellFoundedOn

(***************************************************************************)
(* If we think of R as a less-than relation on S, then the following is    *)
(* the set of elements of S that are less than x.                          *)
(***************************************************************************)
SetLessThan(x, R, S) ==  {y \in S : <<y, x>> \in R}

(***************************************************************************)
             /\ \A y \in LT(x) : SetLessThan(y, R, S) \subseteq LT(x)
             /\ \A y \in LT(x) : LT(y) \subseteq LT(x)
             /\ LT(x) \subseteq S
  \* FIXME: SMT backend fails with exception
  BY Isa DEF SetLessThan, IsTransitivelyClosedOn
<1> HIDE DEF LT  \** from now on, (mostly) use properties in step <1>1 rather than the definition

<1> DEFINE F(x) == CHOOSE g : g = [y \in LT(x) |-> Def(g, y)]
           ff == [x \in S |-> F(x)[x]]
<1> HIDE DEF ff

<1>2. \A x \in S : ff[x] = Def(ff,x)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW x \in S,
                        \A y \in SetLessThan(x, R, S) : ff[y] = Def(ff,y)
                 PROVE  ff[x] = Def(ff,x)
    BY WFInduction, Isa
  <2>2. WFInductiveUnique(LT(x), Def)
    <3>1. LT(x) \subseteq S
      BY <1>1
    <3>2. IsWellFoundedOn(R, LT(x))
      BY <3>1, IsWellFoundedOnSubset
